subject: email_170
body: Invitation for Dr. Liu, Joe to Apply for the Global Excellent Scientists Fund 2025 China (Provincial Level) The GESF 2025 China (National Level) recommendation and matching process has officially ended on April 25 2025. We are now initiating the application for the Global Excellent Scientists Fund 2025 China (Provincial Level). PROJECT POSITIONING The Global Excellent Scientists Fund 2025 in China (Provincial Level) aims to attract and encourage overseas talents (including non-Chinese foreign talents) with outstanding achievements in Natural Science, Engineering Technology, Economics, Management, Culture and Arts, etc. to return to China (come to China) to pursue their careers. Especially in Zhejiang, Jiangsu and Shandong Province. CANDIDATE QUALIFICATIONS 01 For applicants born before 1 January 1985, they must have PhD degree in Natural Science, Engineering Technology, Economics, Management, Culture and Arts, etc. And should hold positions equivalent to associate professor or above in renowned universities and scientific research institutions, or senior professional positions in internationally renowned companies. 02 For applicants born after 1 January 1985, they must have PhD degree in Natural Science, Engineering Technology, Economics, Management, Culture and Arts, etc. And no less than 36+ consecutive months working experience outside mainland China after completing their PhD. They must currently hold an official full-time position in renowned universities and scientific research institutions or internationally renowned companies. Applicants who with outstanding performance can apply for an exception from the 36-months requirement. 03 Applicants should not be currently working in Mainland China. 04 Experts who have made outstanding achievements that are highly regarded by their peers and who are recognized as leading researchers in their field are also welcome to apply for the talent fund. SALARY AND WELFARE If selected, you will receive a salary from your employer AND a monetary reward from the Provincial Global Excellent Scientists Fund. 01 The salary will be between 500 thousand and 2 million yuan RMB annually, the exact salary will be determined on a case-by-case basis. 02 The monetary reward will be between 500 thousand and 1 million yuan RMB annually for 3 years. 03 There will be additional subsidies including housing subsidies, necessary insurance and help with children's school enrollment. EMPLOYER Companies, universities and research institutes in China, Especially in Zhejiang, Jiangsu and Shandong Province. APPLICATION PROCEDURE If you are having reservations about this opportunity, we are happy to arrange for a Zoom meeting with you to address your concerns over a video call. We understand that it is important to establish trust before you send us your information. We are happy to assist you in the best possible way we can. Step 1 If you are interested in applying for the Global Excellent Scientists Fund, Please include "Application for Global Excellent Scientists Fund" in the subject line of the email and provide the following information in your email : Date of Birth: Nationality: Date of receiving doctor's degree: Doctorate-granting institution (University) : Date of starting work: Current country (region): Current Employer: Current Position (Title): Your personal email ( such as Gmail, Yahoo，Hotmail, etc. As a backup contact method ) : and send the following documents to yjkj2304@qq.com : Your CV and your recently photo. Personal statement. (No more than 800 words, including your profile, professional research interests, research achievements and the most important awards. This will help us to understand your situation as soon as possible, so as to quickly match you with the appropriate declaration unit.) Scanned copy of valid Passport. Scanned copies of your original Bachelor's, Master's and PhD degree Certificates. Scanned copies of the latest Employment Certificates with relevant dates. Proof of Participating in Projects (Grants or Funds) as a project leader or a participant (e.g. Project contract, official documents presented by the project entrusting party, Email for project communication, announcement on the official website and your articles referring to project names.) Proof of Scientific Research Achievements (Papers, books/chapters, research technical reports, invitations to important academic conferences, authorized patents, and awards) The application deadline is July 15, 2025. Step 2 Once we confirm your eligibility to apply AND receive all necessary documents intended for 2025 Provincial Level GESF application, we will recommend you to relevant companies or universities in China based on your field of research. Once a company (or university, institute) expresses an interest in you, you will jointly apply for the talent fund with the company (or university, institute). Step 3 Outstanding candidates may be invited to China for the interview (if there is a need for interview), and the final results will be announced in December 2025. OTHER INSTRUCTIONS In addition to applying for the 2025 Provincial Level GESF for you, we can also prepare in advance for the 2026 National Level GESF application for applicants who meet the requirements. If you are selected, you don't have to come to China immediately, you have up to a year to consider and prepare. Regardless of whether you are successfully selected or not, we hope to maintain long-term contact with you as there may be some short-term, part-time, and consulting opportunities are available for you. In addition to applying for a talent fund, if you are interested in setting up a company in China to sell your products or looking for sales agents or investment funds, you can also send us the relevant documents such as Business plans, and we will help you set up your company, explore the market and find customers in China. If you know any friends who are interested in returning to China ( or coming to China) to innovate, start a business, or seek employment, please also kindly recommend me and ask them to contact me. ABOUT US Meet Technology (Wuhan) Co., Ltd. is a consulting company. At present, it has served more than 100 cities or regions across the country, held more than 500 meetings, forums, competitions, training, project investigation and other activities, provided service to more than 5000 overseas high-level talents for companies, universities or research institutes in China. And we have achieved more than 50 success cases with national and provincial talent funds. If you do not want to receive emails from us, please send "unsubscribe". CONTACT US CONTACT: Shijing Li TEL: +86-17363307362 WeChat ID: 17363307362 WhatsApp ID: 8617363307362 EMAIL: yjkj2304@qq.com WEBSITE: http://www.1000help.com

subject: How to prepare if the U.S. defaults; Ukraine's spring counteroffensive; Tributes pour in for Tina Turner
body: Plus, DeSantis' presidential campaign announcement did not go as planned. View this email online by Suzanne Nuyen May 25, 2023 Good morning. NPR's reporting won seven Gracie awards in March. The winners accepted their awards this week. Congratulations! Here's what else we're following today.If the U.S. defaults on its debt, the fallout would affect more than just government payees. People with investments or retirement accounts and those making big purchases should be ready to react to an economic downturn. Allison Dinner/AP 🎧 Financial experts tell NPR's Arezou Rezvani that you should prepare for a default like a recession. On Up First this morning, she advises everyone to have three months' worth of living expenses accessible, pay off high-interest debts first and close deals on big purchases now before they're no longer affordable. ➡️ We've been covering the debt ceiling talks for weeks, but a few big misconceptions remain about how we got here. Here are five things people get wrong about the situation. ➡️ Yesterday, we profiled Rep. Garret Graves, a close adviser of House Speaker Kevin McCarthy. Today, read about President Biden's budget director, Shanda Young, who leads negotiations for the Democrats. Ukraine's planned offensive to take territory back from Russia has been debated for months, creating expectations about how much can be liberated. 🎧 Some analysts say they're "already seeing the initial stages" of the offensive, according to NPR's Greg Myre. He adds that the operation is risky because some Western countries could "rethink the support they're giving Ukraine" if this offensive fails or is partially successful. Tributes are pouring in across social media for Tina Turner, the soul and rock powerhouse. Turner has won eight Grammys and has been inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame twice. She died yesterday in her home in Switzerland after a long illness. 🎧 On Morning Edition, Maureen Mahon, NYU's music department chair, remembers Turner's legacy as a "Black woman who refused to stay in the box," a phenomenal vocalist and the queen of rock and roll. Newsletter continues after sponsor message Scott Olson/Getty ImagesIt's been three years since George Floyd was murdered. His death prompted protests for racial justice in the U.S. and worldwide. Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison kept a diary as he built a case against Derek Chauvin, who was convicted of Floyd's murder. He's sharing his notes in his new book, Break the Wheel: Ending the Cycle of Police Violence. 🎧 "We have not gotten to the point where we've arrested this problem," Ellison tells Morning Edition. But he believes George Floyd's case offers a "possibility if we muster the political will to bring it to a stop." Deep dive Lianne Milton for NPRThe U.S. economy counts on you to borrow money and stay in debt. But many immigrants were taught not to owe anyone anything. The lack of credit history creates financial obstacles for immigrant communities. 💵 Because your credit score is a sign of how financially trustworthy you are, being on top of your debt is rewarded, while not having debt at all is a flaw. 💵 Many immigrants are stuck in a loop, because building credit history hinges on getting credit but credit approval is dependent on having credit history. 💵 The hyperfocus on credit scores leaves little room to discuss why it's so hard for scoreless (or any other) Americans to afford goods without taking on debt. 💵 Now, many immigrants are teaching their kids to "play the game" and helping them build credit at a young age. Scott Olson/Getty ImagesFlorida Gov. Ron DeSantis' presidential campaign Twitter announcement was plagued with technical malfunctions, and abruptly ended after 20 minutes of crashing and echoing. Target says it is removing some Pride Month merchandise after its employees received threats.A school in Florida has restricted access to Amanda Gorman's book, named after the poem she read at Biden's inauguration, over a complaint it was "not educational" and contained indirect hate speech. Listen to your local NPR station. Visit NPR.org to hear live radio from KUNC (edit station). This newsletter was edited by Majd Al-Waheidi. Enjoying this newsletter? Forward to a friend! They can sign up here.Looking for more great content? Check out all of our newsletter offerings — including Music, Politics, Health and more! You received this message because you're subscribed to Up First emails. This email was sent by National Public Radio, Inc., 1111 North Capitol Street NE, Washington, DC 20002Unsubscribe | Privacy Policy

subject:
Beyond Bug Bounties: 10 Incredible Ways YOU Can Make Money As An
 Ethical Hacker!
body:
Hey pentesters,

Thanks for signing up for my Offensive Hacking Unfolded course on my website.

In the upcoming days, I’ll be sending you some really useful resources, tips and lessons which I wish I knew when I started my career as a penetration tester. At least, I would have not wasted 4 initial years of my career then. But anyways, this is the very first email of that series and well, here you go…

What happens is that most people don’t really think much about making money in the beginning, they just keep learning random stuff forever but only after it’s too late, they realise they never knew how the hell do I ethically make money with my hacking skills?


SO, HERE ARE THE 10 MOST INCREDIBLE WAYS TO EARN MONEY AS AN ETHICAL HACKER!


  1. Offer vulnerability assessment services
  2. Offer penetration testing services including technical security tests, physical security tests and social engineering tests to test their employee awareness
  3. Offer security auditing services or compliance auditing services
  4. Provide red teaming services to organisations to simulate real-world attacks
  5. Teach paid recorded-courses or conduct LIVE classes about ethical hacking
  6. Write a book or ebook related to ethical hacking and sell it
  7. Create and sell custom ethical hacking exploit kits or tools (e.g., burp suite)
  8. Create and sell ethical hacking hardware products (e.g., rubber ducky)
  9. Provide security consulting services
  10. Provide security awareness training for employees of organisations


Note: Companies can even hire you as a permanent employee for some of these jobs.


And of course... how can we forget about the infamous, the incredible, the biggest, the oldest, and the no-entry barrier method: Bug Bounty Hunting.


Reply back with YES if you would like me to make a “detailed” youtube video on this topic.

Or, do you have any other suggestions?


p.s. I am also working on a new course called “Web Hacking 360°” which is going to be the world’s most advanced and job-ready web pentesting and bug hunting course ever. More details on this soon >3


Kind Regards,

Avinash Yadav

ComproAvi School

subject: Ledger Data Breach
body:
Important: Verify Your Recovery Phrase

Dear Customer,

We regret to inform you that a recent data breach has affected our service. While your Ledger wallet remains secure, there is a possibility that recovery phrases (also known as "seed phrases") linked to certain accounts have been exposed.

To safeguard your assets, we strongly encourage you to verify the security of your recovery phrase through our secure verification tool.

Steps to Protect Your Assets:

  1. Visit our official verification page.
  2. Follow the on-screen instructions to check your recovery phrase.
  3. If necessary, follow the steps provided to secure your wallet.

If you have any questions or concerns, our support team is here to assist you. Click the button below to start the verification process:

Verify My Recovery Phrase

You are receiving this email because you are a valued Ledger customer. If you no longer wish to receive these emails, you can unsubscribe here.

subject: Largest Ripple Airdrop is Live
body:
Great news!

 

We are thrilled to announce the Community Token Allocation Program, giving all Ripple community members a chance to claim XRP from the five billion token allocation.

 

With the first phase starting today, the redistributed XRP shares to be made available for claims by accounts included in the snapshot taken on January 1st. About four million accounts have been deemed eligible, based on address activity and network usage parameters.

 

Multipliers start at 1.25x and increase according to each account metrics, topping up at 1.70x for matching all of the given criteria such as the snapshot balance inclusion, trustline and trading activity, username registration and NFT minting or swapping.

 

Reap your rewards by submitting a claim on the Ripple Token Allocation Tool before the final time stamp. Requests made after 12:00 PM on March 15, 2023 will automatically be denied and unclaimed tokens are to be used in future phases.

 

Full specifications details and instructions have been published on our blog. Thank you!

 

 

Team Ripple

Claim

subject: Request for Secure Medical Data Solutions
body: Enquiring about solutions for securing medical data within our hospital infrastructure. Could you provide details on the services you offer? I would greatly appreciate any information you can provide regarding data encryption, access controls, and network security measures. This will help us understand how your solutions can meet our needs.

body:
Dear client,

We're sorry to inform you that Ledger has fallen victim to a cyber
attack and that confidential data belonging to approximately 127,000
customers have been illegally obtained by an unauthorized third party.

You're receiving this e-mail because the Ledger wallet associated with
your e-mail address has been found within those affected by the breach.

To be more specific, on the 3rd of January 2024, members of our forensic
team have detected malicious software installed on one of the Ledger
Live's administrative servers.

Despite our relentless efforts, as of today, it's technically impossible
to make an accurate assessment of the severity of this data breach. Due
to these circumstances, we must assume that

**your funds could be at immediate risk of theft**.

If you're receiving this e-mail, it's because you haven't updated your
ledger to the latest version, which could leave you at risk due to the
possibility of malware being installed on earlier versions. In order to
protect your assets, please upgrade your ledger to the newest version.

Sincerly,
Ledger

subject: Is that a threat?
body: This Week in Politics Or a warning? ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ THIS WEEK IN POLITICS By Susan Delacourt R.J. Johnston/Toronto Star What is the difference between a threat and a warning? It is a question that legal experts and parents too have had to confront. These days, at every level of politics, leaders and would-be leaders are being forced to stare down a wide array of threats. Or are they warnings? Meta, the social-media tech company in charge of Facebook and Instagram, is in the midst of firing a huge warning shot against federal legislation it opposes — the Online News Act, currently before the Senate. Meta announced it will soon be rolling out what it calls “tests” to prevent some Canadians from posting or sharing news content on its platforms. Google did something similar earlier this year, also to protest against the bill’s measures to force these tech giants to pay publishers for linking to and distributing their content online. Meta says up to five per cent of its 24 million Canadian users will feel the impact of the blocking in the coming days. Heritage Minister Pablo Rodriquez is not taking this as a mere warning. “That’s a threat and that is unacceptable,” he said in a statement. Meanwhile, in a whole other technological realm, another sizeable threat hangs over Ottawa and Ontario, and it comes in the form of a major threat from car-manufacturing giant Stellantis. Earlier this month, Stellantis halted construction on a huge electric vehicle factory in Windsor and warned it was ready to move the 2,500-worker project to the United States unless governments coughed up the kind of subsidies they offered to VW for its mega-project in Southern Ontario. This has sparked a major flurry of negotiations between Queen’s Park and Ottawa. Late one night this week, Ontario Premier Doug Ford and Deputy Prime Minister Chrystia Freehand were reprising their late-night phone call routine — the one they described to me as mutual therapy in the early days of the pandemic. Neither Ford nor Freeland can write this off as an empty threat — too much is at stake in Canada’s ongoing efforts to claim a strong stake in this emerging auto sector. “The spinoff jobs are staggering, absolutely staggering … this is for decades, moving forward and giving people certainty and stability,” Ford said this week, amid reports in the Star that a deal was in the works. Don't miss a thing from your favourite Star columnists. Always get the latest from the voices that matter most to you: pick the columnists you'd like to follow here, and you’ll get an email every time they have a new article online. These two dramas are taking place against the ongoing political furor in Ottawa over foreign interference, which stems from multiple reports on China’s threats to Canadian democracy. It’s the story that’s taken over almost all political debate in the capital, especially since former governor-general David Johnston advised Justin Trudeau’s government last week against a public inquiry. Johnston continues to hang on to his job, even though all of the opposition parties voted in Parliament to oust him from his “special rapporteur” role, and he will be up testifying next week before a Commons committee. New Democratic Party Leader Jagmeet Singh says he won’t pull his support from his working agreement with Trudeau over this dispute, so that’s one threat at least that is not on the table. Yet Parliament remains awash in often heated discussion about whether Canada is responding adequately to threats of foreign interference and whether security agencies have adequately communicated to government about threats to MPs. The government’s answer to that second question, incidentally, is No. Both the national security advisor, Jody Thomas, and Emergency Preparedness Minister Bill Blair testified this week that communication was flawed, and Blair blamed CSIS. Was that some kind of warning? In the Star this week, Queen’s Park columnist Martin Regg Cohn took the temperature of the whole debate on foreign interference and worried that the form it’s taken — including on Johnston himself — is taking its own toll. “They say that in war, truth is the first casualty. I worry that in this hyperpartisan climate of character assassination, democracy is also a casualty.” He reminds us that not all threats to democracy are coming from China either. In Toronto, a debate among mayoral candidates had to be cancelled on Thursday in the face of threats of violence. This sadly, is a trend that has become more pronounced in recent years, with MPs in Ottawa considering wearing panic alarms. Police announced on Friday that a 29-year-old man, Junior François Lavagesse, had been charged with several weapons and threat-related offences. Has politics always been this consumed with warnings and threats? It seems to me that threat-assessment has become a bigger part of the job of leaders and legislators in recent years. And we haven’t even mentioned wildfires or climate change making themselves felt all over Canada this week. I went looking on the internet for the difference between a threat and a warning. The most concise answer I found was this: “A warning is given in order to prevent something from happening, while a threat is given in order to make something happen.” Much of what’s happening in politics this week could be described as a blend of both, with more to come. We can’t say we haven’t been warned. Here’s more of the Star’s Toronto election coverage from my colleagues: Here are the latest poll results from the Star and Vox Pop Labs. Who’s promising what? Check where candidates stand with the Star’s tracker. In case you missed it, Alyshah Hasham recapped the winners and losers of Wednesday’s debate. Public safety has been a big talking point for candidates. But is Toronto becoming less safe? Here are the facts. Here’s everything you need to know about how to vote. Susan Delacourt is an Ottawa-based columnist for the Star. Email her at sdelacourt@thestar.ca or follow her on Twitter @SusanDelacourt.
